headline: Public Transport Ridership Stalls at 22% Modal Share in KL
context: Prasarana reported that public transport modal share in Greater KL remained at 22% in 2025, unchanged since 2019 despite RM62 billion invested in MRT1, MRT2, and LRT extensions over the previous decade.

cards.big: RM62 billion on rail but modal share stuck at 22% — 78% of KL commuters still choose cars over trains
cards.sub: Last-mile connectivity failure means 42% of rail users still drive to stations — trains don't reach where people live

cards.big: Prasarana accumulated RM42 billion in debt with annual losses of RM2.8 billion — no public transport system in ASEAN performs worse financially
cards.sub: Farebox recovery ratio at 32% means government subsidises 68 sen of every ringgit in operating costs

cards.big: Bus network covers only 35% of Greater KL residential areas — 2.8 million residents have no bus stop within 800m of home
cards.sub: Low-income communities in Sentul, Cheras Selatan, and Klang are most underserved by existing rail and bus routes

cards.big: 4 separate agencies — Prasarana, KTMB, SPAD successor APAD, and state bus operators — manage public transport with no unified authority
cards.sub: Integrated fare system promised since 2015 still not implemented — commuters pay separate fares for each transfer

cards.big: Building expensive rail lines without solving the last-mile problem is like building airports without roads to reach them
Should KL adopt a single transport authority with unified fares and mandatory last-mile bus coverage within 400m of every rail station?
